stories.title: Russell Henley Wins Charles Schwab Challenge in Playoff Over Eric Cole

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
RUSSELL HENLEY wins the Schwab Challenge in dramatic fashion.

Down to Cole going into 16 — then: birdie, birdie, birdie to force a playoff.

One more birdie on 18 in sudden death.

Four consecutive birdies to win. $1.78M. 🏆

#PGATour #SchwabChallenge

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Eric Cole led most of the final round at Colonial.

Then Russell Henley made 4 birdies in a row — three to force OT, one to win it.

That's how you finish a tournament. Heartbreak for Cole.

#PGATour #SchwabChallenge

facebook.long_form:
Russell Henley did something special on Sunday at Colonial Country Club, and golf fans who turned off their TVs early nearly missed one of the year's great finishes.

Henley entered the back nine on Sunday trailing Eric Cole, who had played an excellent final round and appeared to have the Charles Schwab Challenge in hand. But Henley launched a birdie sequence that rewrote the afternoon entirely. He birdied No. 16. Then No. 17. Then No. 18 — erasing the deficit and forcing a sudden-death playoff with Cole.

In the playoff on the 18th hole, Henley made a fourth consecutive birdie to take the title. Four birdies in a row. $1,782,000. And a victory that will be remembered at Colonial for a long time.

Cole finishes T2 in one of the year's toughest heartbreaks. Mac Meissner, Alex Smalley, and defending champion Ben Griffin share T3.
facebook.image_caption: Russell Henley wins the 2026 Schwab Challenge with FOUR consecutive birdies — three to force the playoff, one to win it. 🏆 Cole falls heartbreakingly short in OT. #PGATour #SchwabChallenge

article.headline: Russell Henley Makes Four Straight Birdies to Win the 2026 Schwab Challenge in Dramatic Playoff

stories.title: Niemann Wins LIV Golf Korea in Playoff — Claims Record 8th LIV Title

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Joaquin Niemann wins LIV Golf Korea in a playoff over Talor Gooch.

Both at -12. Niemann birdied 18 in sudden death to win.

That's career LIV win No. 8 — the most in league HISTORY.

3 titles ahead of anyone else. 🏆 #LIVGolf #LIVGolfKorea

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Crushers GC wins the LIV Golf Korea team title at -23.

DeChambeau (T3 individually) anchors yet another Crushers team win.

Meanwhile Niemann (8 wins) and Gooch (playoff loss) continue as two of LIV's most consistent players.

#LIVGolf #CrushersGC

facebook.long_form:
Joaquin Niemann has done something no player has done in the history of LIV Golf — and he did it on the 18th hole at Asiad Country Club in Busan, South Korea.

With Talor Gooch matching him step for step through 54 holes at 12-under par, Niemann and Gooch went to sudden death on Sunday evening. On the par-4 18th, Niemann drained a birdie to walk away with the title. The win — his eighth in LIV Golf competition — makes him the most prolific individual winner in the league's history. He now stands three victories clear of any other player.

The team competition also produced a winner: Bryson DeChambeau's Crushers GC posted a combined 23-under to win the Korea team trophy by three shots. DeChambeau himself finished T3 individually.

For Gooch, a playoff loss is a bitter outcome after posting the kind of week that would win most tournaments. But Niemann — already a force before this — is now in the business of making LIV history.
facebook.image_caption: Niemann wins LIV Korea in a playoff over Gooch. Career win No. 8 — the most in LIV Golf HISTORY. 🏆 Crushers GC claims the team trophy. #LIVGolf #Niemann

article.headline: Niemann Makes LIV Golf History in Korea Playoff — 8th Title, Most All-Time

stories.title: Memorial Tournament Preview — Scheffler Chases Historic Three-Peat

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Memorial Tournament is this week. (June 4-7, Muirfield Village)

Scottie Scheffler is going for a THREE-PEAT.

Only Tiger Woods (1999-2001) has ever done it at Jack's place.

$20M purse. 9 of top-10 in the world. #PGATour #MemorialTournament

facebook.long_form:
The golf world turns its attention to Columbus, Ohio, this week for The Memorial Tournament — one of the most prestigious non-major events on the PGA Tour calendar, and the one that comes with a $20 million purse.

Scottie Scheffler arrives as the defending champion — and as the man chasing history. He has won The Memorial in back-to-back years, becoming only the second player ever to win consecutive editions at Muirfield Village. The question this week is whether he can become the third player ever to win it three times in a row. The first was Tiger Woods, who completed the trifecta in 2001.

Nine of the world's top-10 players are in the field. Rory McIlroy and Cameron Young join Scheffler as projected favorites. Jack Nicklaus' tournament, at Jack Nicklaus' course, with one of the strongest fields in golf.

The Memorial runs June 4-7. Coverage on Golf Channel and NBC.
facebook.image_caption: The Memorial Tournament is HERE. Scheffler chasing history — only Tiger Woods (1999-2001) has ever won three in a row at Muirfield Village. $20M purse. 9 of top-10 in world. June 4-7. #MemorialTournament #PGATour

article.headline: Scottie Scheffler Chases Memorial Three-Peat as Nine of World's Top 10 Head to Muirfield Village

stories.title: US Women's Open Preview — Korda and Thitikul at Historic Riviera

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
The US Women's Open is also this week — at Riviera CC for the FIRST TIME EVER.

Korda: 3 wins in 2026, 17 career titles. The favorite.
Thitikul: World No. 1, zero majors. Still searching.

History at Riviera. June 4-7. #USWomensOpen #LPGA

facebook.long_form:
Riviera Country Club has hosted some of the most iconic moments in golf history — Los Angeles Opens, U.S. Opens for the men. But on Thursday, June 4, it hosts a women's major championship for the first time ever. The 81st US Women's Open Presented by Ally arrives at Pacific Palisades.

The field is extraordinary. All 25 of the world's top-25 players are entered. The USGA received 1,897 entries. And the headliner matchup everyone wants to see has set up perfectly.

Nelly Korda arrives as the tournament favorite. Three wins already in 2026, including the Chevron Championship for her third major title and 17th career LPGA victory. She plays aggressive, smart, championship golf.

Standing across the tee from her in the marquee story of the week: World No. 1 Jeeno Thitikul. Thitikul won back-to-back CME Group Tour Championships in 2024 and 2025. She is the best player in women's golf without a major championship to her name — and she is determined to change that this week.

US Women's Open. Riviera CC. June 4-7.
facebook.image_caption: The US Women's Open is at Riviera CC for the FIRST TIME EVER. Korda (3 wins in 2026, Chevron champion) vs Thitikul (World No. 1, zero majors). History is on the line. June 4-7. #USWomensOpen #LPGA

article.headline: US Women's Open Heads to Riviera for Historic First — Korda and Thitikul Set for Title Showdown

stories.title: Nelly Korda's 2026 LPGA Dominance — Three Wins and Counting

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda's 2026 LPGA season so far:

✅ 3 wins
✅ Chevron Championship (her 3rd major)
✅ 17 career LPGA titles
✅ World No. 1 ranking

Now comes the US Women's Open at Riviera.

Can she add win No. 4 — and major No. 4? #LPGA #NellyKorda

facebook.long_form:
At 28 years old, Nelly Korda is entering the conversation about the greatest runs in women's golf history — and her 2026 season makes it very hard to ignore.

She enters June with three victories: the Chevron Championship (her third major and 17th career LPGA title), the Riviera Maya Open, and a third win in 2026. That is three wins from the year's biggest events. She has not just been winning — she has been winning the ones that matter most.

The comparison point that keeps emerging: Annika Sorenstam, whose 2001 and 2005 seasons redefined what dominance in women's golf looked like. Korda does not shy from the comparison, but she is focused on what comes next rather than what the record books say about her.

What comes next is the US Women's Open at Riviera, June 4-7 — a tournament she would dearly love to add to her collection. A fourth win at the year's biggest stage. Another major. Another step in what is becoming a career for the ages.
facebook.image_caption: Nelly Korda in 2026: 3 wins, 1 major (Chevron), 17 career LPGA titles. Now comes the US Women's Open at Riviera — can she make it 4 wins and 2 majors in one year? #LPGA #NellyKorda #USWomensOpen

article.headline: Nelly Korda Is Running Away With 2026 — Three Wins, a Third Major, and the US Women's Open Ahead
